member.name: T:System.Firmware.BootService.Protocols.CdRomProtocol
member.summary: The CD-ROM Media Device Path is used to define a system partition that exists on a CD-ROM. The CD-ROM is assumed to contain an ISO-9660 file system and follow the CD-ROM “El Torito” format https://uefi.org/specs/UEFI/2.10/10_Protocols_Device_Path_Protocol.html#cd-rom-media-device-path

member.name: M:System.Firmware.BootService.Protocols.CdRomProtocol.#ctor
member.summary: The CD-ROM Media Device Path is used to define a system partition that exists on a CD-ROM. The CD-ROM is assumed to contain an ISO-9660 file system and follow the CD-ROM “El Torito” format https://uefi.org/specs/UEFI/2.10/10_Protocols_Device_Path_Protocol.html#cd-rom-media-device-path

member.name: P:System.Firmware.BootService.Protocols.CdRomProtocol.BootEntry
member.summary: Boot Entry number from the Boot Catalog. The Initial/Default entry is defined as zero.

member.name: P:System.Firmware.BootService.Protocols.CdRomProtocol.PartitionStart
member.summary: Starting RBA of the partition on the medium. CD-ROMs use Relative logical Block Addressing.

member.name: P:System.Firmware.BootService.Protocols.CdRomProtocol.PartitionSize
member.summary: Size of the partition in units of Blocks, also called Sectors.

member.name: M:System.Firmware.BootService.Protocols.CdRomProtocol.#ctor(System.UInt32,System.UInt64,System.UInt64)
member.summary: Create new protocol instance from boot entry and partition offset

member.name: T:System.Firmware.BootService.Protocols.FilePathProtocol
member.summary: File Path Media Device Path https://uefi.org/specs/UEFI/2.10/10_Protocols_Device_Path_Protocol.html#file-path-media-device-path

member.name: M:System.Firmware.BootService.Protocols.FilePathProtocol.#ctor
member.summary: File Path Media Device Path https://uefi.org/specs/UEFI/2.10/10_Protocols_Device_Path_Protocol.html#file-path-media-device-path

member.name: P:System.Firmware.BootService.Protocols.FilePathProtocol.PathName
member.summary: A NULL-terminated Path string including directory and file name.

member.name: M:System.Firmware.BootService.Protocols.FilePathProtocol.#ctor(System.String)
member.summary: Create new protocol instance from file path

member.name: T:System.Firmware.BootService.Protocols.PartitionFormat
member.summary: Partition format

member.name: F:System.Firmware.BootService.Protocols.PartitionFormat.LegacyMBR
member.summary: Mastre boot record

member.name: F:System.Firmware.BootService.Protocols.PartitionFormat.GuidPartitionTable
member.summary: Guid partition table

member.name: T:System.Firmware.BootService.Protocols.SignatureType
member.summary: Signature type

member.name: F:System.Firmware.BootService.Protocols.SignatureType.NoDiskSignature
member.summary: Raw

member.name: F:System.Firmware.BootService.Protocols.SignatureType.MbrSignature
member.summary: Mastre boot record

member.name: F:System.Firmware.BootService.Protocols.SignatureType.GuidSignature
member.summary: Guid partition table

member.name: T:System.Firmware.BootService.Protocols.HardDriveProtocol
member.summary: The Hard Drive Media Device Path is used to represent a partition on a hard drive.

member.name: M:System.Firmware.BootService.Protocols.HardDriveProtocol.#ctor
member.summary: The Hard Drive Media Device Path is used to represent a partition on a hard drive.

member.name: P:System.Firmware.BootService.Protocols.HardDriveProtocol.PartitionNumber
member.summary: Describes the entry in a partition table, starting with entry 1. Partition number zero represents the entire device. Valid partition numbers for a MBR partition are [1, 4]. Valid partition numbers for a GPT partition are [1, NumberOfPar titionEntries].

member.name: P:System.Firmware.BootService.Protocols.HardDriveProtocol.PartitionStart
member.summary: Starting LBA of the partition on the hard drive

member.name: P:System.Firmware.BootService.Protocols.HardDriveProtocol.PartitionSize
member.summary: Size of the partition in units of Logical Blocks

member.name: P:System.Firmware.BootService.Protocols.HardDriveProtocol.GptPartitionSignature
member.summary: Signature unique to this partition, this field contains a 16 byte signature.

member.name: P:System.Firmware.BootService.Protocols.HardDriveProtocol.PartitionFormat
member.summary: Partition Format: (Unused values reserved) 0x01 - PC-AT compatible legacy MBR (Legacy MBR) . Partition Start and Partition Size come from Parti tionStartingLBA and PartitionSizeInLBA for the partition.0x02—GUID Partition Table

member.name: P:System.Firmware.BootService.Protocols.HardDriveProtocol.SignatureType
member.summary: Type of Disk Signature: (Unused values reserved) 0x00 - No Disk Signature. 0x01 - 32-bit signature from address 0x1b8 of the type 0x01 MBR. 0x02 - GUID signature.

member.name: M:System.Firmware.BootService.Protocols.HardDriveProtocol.#ctor(System.Firmware.Win32Native.PARTITION_INFORMATION_EX)
member.summary: Create new protocol instance from structure

member.name: M:System.Firmware.BootService.Protocols.HardDriveProtocol.#ctor(System.Guid)
member.summary: Create a protocol from unique partition

member.name: T:System.Firmware.BootService.Protocols.MediaDeviceProtocolType
member.summary: https://uefi.org/specs/UEFI/2.10/10_Protocols_Device_Path_Protocol.html#media-device-path

member.name: F:System.Firmware.BootService.Protocols.MediaDeviceProtocolType.HardDrive
member.summary: The Hard Drive Media Device Path is used to represent a partition on a hard drive. Each partition has at least Hard Drive Device Path node, each describing an entry in a partition table. EFI supports MBR and GPT partitioning formats. Partitions are numbered according to their entry in their respective partition table, starting with 1. Partitions are addressed in EFI starting at LBA zero. A partition number of zero can be used to represent the raw hard drive or a raw extended partition

member.name: F:System.Firmware.BootService.Protocols.MediaDeviceProtocolType.CdRom
member.summary: The CD-ROM Media Device Path is used to define a system partition that exists on a CD-ROM. The CD-ROM is assumed to contain an ISO-9660 file system and follow the CD-ROM “El Torito” format. The Boot Entry number from the Boot Catalog is how the “El Torito” specification defines the existence of bootable entities on a CD-ROM. In EFI the bootable entity is an EFI System Partition that is pointed to by the Boot Entry.

member.name: F:System.Firmware.BootService.Protocols.MediaDeviceProtocolType.Vendor
member.summary: Vendor-Defined Media Device Path

member.name: F:System.Firmware.BootService.Protocols.MediaDeviceProtocolType.FilePath
member.summary: A NULL-terminated Path string including directory and file names. The length of this string n can be determined by subtracting 4 from the Length entry. A device path may contain one or more of these nodes. Each node can optionally add a “" separator to the beginning and/or the end of the Path Name string. The complete path to a file can be found by logically concatenating all the Path Name strings in the File Path Media Device Path nodes. This is typically used to describe the directory path in one node, and the filename in another node.

member.name: F:System.Firmware.BootService.Protocols.MediaDeviceProtocolType.Media
member.summary: The Media Protocol Device Path is used to denote the protocol that is being used in a device path at the location of the path specified. Many protocols are inherent to the style of device path.

member.name: F:System.Firmware.BootService.Protocols.MediaDeviceProtocolType.PiwgFirmwareFile
member.summary: This type is used by systems implementing the UEFI PI Specification to describe a firmware file. The exact format and usage are defined in that specification.

member.name: F:System.Firmware.BootService.Protocols.MediaDeviceProtocolType.PiwgFirmwareVolume
member.summary: This type is used by systems implementing the UEFI PI Specification to describe a firmware volume. The exact format and usage are defined in that specification.

member.name: F:System.Firmware.BootService.Protocols.MediaDeviceProtocolType.RelativeOffsetRange
member.summary: This device path node specifies a range of offsets relative to the first byte available on the device. The starting offset is the first byte of the range and the ending offset is the last byte of the range (not the last byte + 1).

member.name: F:System.Firmware.BootService.Protocols.MediaDeviceProtocolType.RamDisk
member.summary: This device path node specifies a range of offsets relative to the end of the device. The starting offset is the first byte of the range and the ending offset is the last byte of the range (not the last byte + 1).

member.name: T:System.Firmware.BootService.Protocols.MediaProtocol
member.summary: The Media Protocol Device Path is used to denote the protocol that is being used in a device path at the location of the path specified. Many protocols are inherent to the style of device path. https://uefi.org/specs/UEFI/2.10/10_Protocols_Device_Path_Protocol.html#media-protocol-device-path

member.name: M:System.Firmware.BootService.Protocols.MediaProtocol.#ctor
member.summary: The Media Protocol Device Path is used to denote the protocol that is being used in a device path at the location of the path specified. Many protocols are inherent to the style of device path. https://uefi.org/specs/UEFI/2.10/10_Protocols_Device_Path_Protocol.html#media-protocol-device-path

member.name: P:System.Firmware.BootService.Protocols.MediaProtocol.ProtocolGUID
member.summary: The ID of the protocol

member.name: M:System.Firmware.BootService.Protocols.MediaProtocol.#ctor(System.Guid)
member.summary: Create new protocol instance from protocol GUID identificator

member.name: T:System.Firmware.BootService.Protocols.VendorDefinedProtocol
member.summary: Vendor-Defined Media Device Path https://uefi.org/specs/UEFI/2.10/10_Protocols_Device_Path_Protocol.html#vendor-defined-media-device-path

member.name: M:System.Firmware.BootService.Protocols.VendorDefinedProtocol.#ctor
member.summary: Vendor-Defined Media Device Path https://uefi.org/specs/UEFI/2.10/10_Protocols_Device_Path_Protocol.html#vendor-defined-media-device-path

member.name: P:System.Firmware.BootService.Protocols.VendorDefinedProtocol.VendorGuid
member.summary: Vendor-assigned GUID that defines the data that follows

member.name: P:System.Firmware.BootService.Protocols.VendorDefinedProtocol.VendorDefinedData
member.summary: Vendor-defined variable size data.
